title: Golden Smoky Middle Eastern Lamb Mansaf
description: A smoky and crispy Middle Eastern dish featuring ground nutmeg, served in a corn husk wrap, perfect for a sick day. This jain-friendly mansaf is a hearty and comforting meal.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large pan, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the ground lamb and cook until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Stir in the ground nutmeg, salt, and black pepper.
Spoon the lamb mixture onto the center of each corn husk.
Fold the corn husks over the filling to form a triangle or square shape, and secure with a toothpick if needed.
Place the mansaf on a baking sheet and bake for 20 minutes, or until the corn husks are crispy and golden brown.
